context: Defines minimal stable-surface contracts for redaction, contract completeness profiles, read-model freshness and watermarks, command receipt event triads, event correction and supersession, root and module implementation topology, implementation evidence, implementation acceptance criteria, decision provenance, schema version compatibility, adapter-facing capability minimums, and capability negotiation re-triage.
assumptions: Stable and adapter-facing surfaces need explicit consumption contracts before external reliance
Root topology should become schema-backed before module linkage and runtime reality checks
Adapter capability posture should be explicit before remote integrations are described as stable
Write-capable surfaces need audit linkage before command semantics are exposed as stable
Append-only event surfaces need correction semantics before consumers treat event history as stable truth

sections.content: Sensitive-surface governance is metadata-first. AODS does not infer private data by scanning text, and it does not perform runtime redaction. A surface declares whether sensitive payloads can appear, which sensitive classes apply, and which redaction posture governs broader exposure. sensitive_classes is limited to pii, credentials, medical, financial, proprietary, regulated, secret, or other. redaction_posture is none, omit, redact, handle-only, or debug-only. none means no sensitive payload is expected. omit means the sensitive field or section must not appear in the exposed surface. redact means a value may appear only after irreversible masking. handle-only means a stable surface may carry an opaque handle but not the underlying secret or personal value. debug-only means the data may exist only in local diagnostic or evidence surfaces and must not become stable public output. When has_sensitive_payloads is true, sensitive_classes and exposure_policy are mandatory because a boolean alone cannot tell an agent how to avoid leakage. This model is intentionally not a privacy framework. It is a trust-boundary marker that keeps stable consumption from treating sensitive examples, debug traces, credentials, customer identifiers, or regulated values as ordinary reusable documentation. The negative validation strategy is deterministic: a module that marks has_sensitive_payloads=true without sensitive_classes or exposure_policy fails validation, and manifest/module redaction summaries must mirror when either side declares them.

A contract completeness profile declares the minimum information required before a surface can be consumed as stable. v0.7 defines only three profiles. read-model covers read-only data surfaces such as generated indexes, schema views, summaries, or inventory outputs. It must identify the data source, field set, freshness posture, identity or key semantics, and failure or absence semantics. Stable read-model contracts must declare read_model.freshness with snapshot_id, exported_at, source_watermark, and staleness so consumers can tell which source snapshot they are reading. staleness may be current, stale, partial, or unknown. partial snapshots must explain partial_semantics or partial_reason before stable consumption. command covers action surfaces such as CLI commands, hooks, workflows, or agent-triggered operations. It must identify input shape, side effects, preconditions, authorization or actor boundary, idempotency posture, observable outputs, and failure semantics. Stable write-capable command surfaces should also link command, receipt, and event or projection semantics before external consumers rely on writes. implementation-linkage covers the design-to-reality bridge used by the topology pilot. It must identify implementation repository, path or path pattern, implementation status, optional PR or issue references, and the authority surface that owns the claim. Profiles do not define every possible field in the ecosystem. They define the lowest safe floor for stable consumption, so a consumer can tell whether it is reading data, invoking action, or following a design-to-implementation link. A stable surface without a profile remains under-specified even when prose is clear. The validator boundary for this round is deterministic and metadata based: read-model profile must declare freshness and watermark metadata; manifest/module contract summaries still mirror the profile only.

A write-capable stable surface should describe its command, receipt, and event or projection linkage before agents or adapters depend on it. command means the requested write intent: actor boundary, source, target, payload schema, schema version, preconditions, idempotency key, side effects, and authorization posture. receipt means the durable attempt record: receipt_id, command_ref, status, accepted_at or rejected_at, rejection_category, observable_outputs, and audit_anchor. event or projection means the observable state consequence: event_ref or projection_ref, emitted_at or materialized_at, schema version, ordering key, replay posture, and consumer guidance. triad linkage means the command points to the receipt, the receipt points back to the command and forward to any event or projection, and the event or projection can be traced to the receipt for audit and replay. Missing pieces may be declared as not-applicable only with an authority reason and consumer guidance. This boundary does not execute commands, require an event bus runtime, define correction or supersession semantics, create a general workflow engine, or guarantee exactly-once delivery. It only prevents stable write-capable surfaces from relying on prose-only auditability.

An append-only event surface must not silently rewrite historical truth. Stable event consumers need explicit correction and supersession semantics before event history, timelines, or projections are treated as authoritative. correction_event means a new event that names correction_of, correction_actor, correction_source, correction_reason, corrected_fields, and replacement event posture without mutating the original event. supersession_link means an event may declare supersedes, superseded_by, replacement_event, and effective_at so consumers can identify which record currently carries the preferred truth. retraction means a correction class that states retraction_reason, retracted_event, replacement_event when one exists, and whether consumers should hide, mark, or preserve the original event. projection guidance means the authority surface tells readers how corrected events should appear in read models, timelines, exports, and audit views; it distinguishes historical, advisory, blocking, and replacement posture. This boundary does not allow history rewrite, implement an event store runtime, run automatic replay, migrate historical data, or decide domain truth by itself. Human review still owns whether the correction reason is adequate and whether a projection can safely consume the corrected event.

sections.topic: project topology and implementation alignment pilot
sections.content: The project_topology pilot exists to break design-only green loops. AODS validation can be fully green while a separate implementation repository is absent, stalled, or invisible. The pilot turns that external reality into a first-class root surface before it becomes a runtime gate. Root topology now lives at manifest.project_topology. It declares the design repository posture plus implementation_repos[]. Each repo entry carries id, locator, role, branch, status, and optional notes. design_repo uses the same minimum shape so the corpus can declare where authority lives before checking downstream implementation paths. Status is a claim about implementation reality, not proof by itself. Allowed status values for the pilot are not-started, planned, in-progress, partial, current, blocked, deprecated, and removed. Module implementation linkage now lives at module.meta.implementation and mirrors through manifest.modules[].implementation. It points from a module to repo_id, paths, status, optional pr_refs, authority_surface, evidence anchors, and acceptance criteria. pr_refs are trace anchors and do not replace path linkage. Paths are relative to the linked implementation repository. Evidence anchors live only in module.meta.implementation.evidence[]. Acceptance criteria live only in module.meta.implementation.acceptance_criteria[] and link contract requirements to evidence refs, validator rules, fixtures, or manual review posture. The manifest carries evidence_summary and acceptance_summary only. evidence_summary counts total, current, planned, stale, and blocked evidence. acceptance_summary counts total, satisfied, planned, partial, waived, blocked, and manual_review criteria. Current implementation linkage must declare at least one evidence anchor and one acceptance criterion so current status is reviewable and tied to a requirement. Evidence locators and criteria are declarative; validators may check repo-relative evidence locator existence under validate --reality when the linked repository root is resolvable, but validators do not execute evidence commands. validate --reality now emits a topology summary with linked_modules, unlinked_modules, checked_paths, missing_paths, evidence_total, checked_evidence_locators, missing_evidence_locators, and explicit unchecked_reason when repo locators cannot yet be resolved from the selected repo root. Non-goals remain cross-repo CI dispatch, automatic code ownership detection, fingerprint drift gates, arbitrary command execution, and closing issue 28 as duplicate. The staged path now reads: U-007 defined pilot semantics, U-008 landed root schema plus examples, U-009 landed module linkage schema and mirror rules, U-010 landed topology-aware reality summaries with explicit partial-check reporting, U-027 landed declarative implementation evidence, and U-029 landed implementation acceptance criteria.

Schema versioning defines how stable consumers survive change. AODS has three version layers: aods_v describes the framework contract, sys_v describes the corpus contract, and module.v describes one module contract. Stable and adapter-facing surfaces need a breaking_policy because consumers may cache, generate, or automate against them. strict means breaking changes are not accepted without a larger framework or corpus compatibility decision. versioned means breaking changes are allowed only with a declared compatibility window and migration guidance. experimental means breaking changes are allowed without a compatibility promise, and it is valid only for experimental, internal, or local-only surfaces. Breaking changes include removing a field, changing required or optional status at a stable boundary, changing type, removing enum values, changing field meaning, or changing side-effect semantics. Non-breaking changes include adding optional fields, clarifying prose, adding examples, adding non-authoritative evidence, or tightening validation wording without changing consumer behavior. Deprecated is a compatibility holding state; removed is terminal. A deprecated stable surface must name a replacement, compatibility window, or removal guidance before removed state. The minimal validation strategy is deterministic: manifest/module schema_versioning summaries must mirror when declared, and validation-rules records the future strict gate for breaking changes without building a migration engine in this round.

adapter-facing does not mean general negotiation. v0.7 only defines the minimum contract needed before a surface may claim stable adapter capability. An adapter-facing capability entry must identify adapter_id, capability_id, transport_scope, contract_profile, schema_version_policy, freshness_posture, redaction_posture, and at least one audit_anchor. transport_scope says whether the capability is local-only, hosted, or hybrid. contract_profile ties the adapter claim back to read-model, command, or a later explicit capability profile instead of inventing ad-hoc surface meanings. schema_version_policy tells consumers whether compatibility is strict, versioned, or experimental. freshness_posture tells whether the adapter claim is static, on-demand, or continuously refreshed. audit_anchor points at the authority or evidence surface that can justify the capability claim. Non-goals for this round are negotiation handshake formats, provider discovery, auth exchange, transport retries, fallback ranking, and dynamic capability probing. The purpose is narrower: prevent adapter-facing surfaces from becoming stable by vague prose alone.

Capability negotiation remains a deferred runtime protocol. The v0.8 re-triage defines only metadata boundaries so adapter-facing claims can be compared without creating a handshake. provider capability means a provider declares capability_id, contract_profile, schema_version_policy, transport_scope, freshness_posture, redaction_posture, limits, and evidence_anchor. consumer requirement means a consumer declares required capability_id, accepted contract_profile, required schema_version_policy, transport_scope needs, freshness requirement, redaction floor, and blocking posture. compatibility matching means deterministic metadata comparison of provider capability to consumer requirement; it can say compatible, incompatible, partial, or unknown. evidence link means provider and consumer claims can point at implementation evidence, acceptance criteria, fixture manifests, or authority sections. This re-triage does not introduce negotiation sessions, runtime discovery, auth exchange, provider selection, fallback ranking, dynamic probing, remote execution, or transport retries. If a future task needs those behaviors, it must start from these metadata fields and add a separate protocol surface instead of overloading adapter-facing prose.

Agent-consumable decisions are stable only when their source boundary is explicit. A decision artifact may declare decision_provenance to identify whether the decision is consumed by agents, held for human review, or internal. The basis states whether the decision comes directly from source evidence, from a derived summary, or from a mixed boundary. source_refs must point at sections or artifacts that authorize the decision. evidence_refs may point at proof anchors. summary_ref is required when the decision is derived from a summary or mixed with a summary because agents must know where synthesis happened. evidence_status is current, stale, unresolved, or withheld. consumption_gate is stable, review-required, blocked, or experimental. An agent-consumable decision cannot be stable when evidence is stale, unresolved, or withheld. Stale evidence means reviewers must refresh or downgrade before stable consumption. Unresolved evidence means agents must not treat the decision as authoritative. Withheld evidence means a stable consumer needs another allowed authority or an explicit review gate. This boundary does not create a full provenance framework, evidence warehouse, or cross-corpus resolver. It only prevents derived decision summaries from becoming stable agent instructions without source and evidence posture.

The stable-surface contract layer is deliberately small. It adds explicit metadata, profile names, read-model freshness and watermark metadata, command receipt event audit linkage, event correction and supersession vocabulary, root topology semantics, compatibility language, decision provenance boundaries, a trimmed adapter capability floor, and metadata-only capability matching boundaries. It does not add automatic sensitive-data detection, redaction transforms, role-based access control, migration code generation, semantic schema diffing, multi-version negotiation, cross-repo fetch, evidence warehouse behavior, implementation fingerprinting, command execution, event bus runtime, event store replay, historical data migration, runtime provider discovery, auth exchange, fallback ranking, or dynamic capability probing. It also does not make every future profile stable. full write-model runtime, resource ownership, export, generated artifact, and full adapter negotiation profiles remain deferred. The model favors deterministic sensors before broad inference. Required metadata can be checked by schema and validator. Human review still owns the meaning of a redaction policy, the sufficiency of a contract profile, whether a topology status is honest, whether a read-model source watermark is semantically sufficient, whether command receipt event linkage is operationally sufficient, whether an event correction reason is adequate, whether a decision summary is faithful to evidence, whether an adapter capability claim is adequately evidenced, and whether a partial capability match is acceptable. This boundary protects AODS from expanding into a general application governance framework before it has closed the trust gaps that block v0.7.

artifacts.usage: Canonical redaction posture values for stable-surface risk review.

rows: stable consumption allowed when other authority gates pass
sensitive payload must be excluded from exposed surface
stable output cannot carry the field or section
sensitive value may appear only after masking
stable output must not preserve original value
opaque reference may appear without underlying value
stable output may carry handles but not secrets
payload is local diagnostic or evidence material

artifacts.usage: Minimum field guide for v0.7 stable contract completeness profiles.

rows: source, fields, identity, freshness, absence_semantics, failure_semantics
input_shape, side_effects, preconditions, actor_boundary, idempotency, outputs, failure_semantics
action, workflow, CLI, hook, or agent operation

artifacts.usage: Minimum freshness and watermark fields for stable read-model contracts.

rows: authority or data source behind the read model
stable fields exposed by the read model
key or identity semantics for records or generated entries
stable identifier for the exported snapshot
timestamp or deterministic build time for the snapshot
source-side watermark, commit, offset, data version, or equivalent freshness anchor
current, stale, partial, or unknown currentness posture
how consumers should interpret partial or lagging snapshots
meaning of missing, empty, or withheld data
how generation or read failures are represented

artifacts.usage: Minimum fields for write-capable command receipt event triads.

rows: command_id, actor_boundary, source, target, payload_schema, schema_version, preconditions, idempotency_key, side_effects, authorization
receipt_id, command_ref, status, accepted_at or rejected_at, rejection_category, observable_outputs, audit_anchor
event_ref or projection_ref, receipt_ref, emitted_at or materialized_at, schema_version, ordering_key, replay_posture, consumer_guidance
command_ref, receipt_ref, event_ref or projection_ref, not_applicable_reason, authority_surface

artifacts.usage: Explicit deferred surfaces for write-capable command receipt event triads.

rows: executing writes requires runtime authorization, side-effect isolation, and rollback semantics beyond static contract metadata
durable delivery and projection materialization require infrastructure-specific semantics
correction, retraction, and supersession policy belongs to append-only event semantics
delivery guarantees depend on runtime transport, storage, and idempotency implementation

artifacts.usage: Minimum fields for append-only event correction and supersession boundaries.

rows: event_id, correction_of, correction_actor, correction_source, correction_reason, corrected_fields, replacement_event
new event records the correction without mutating original history
current preferred truth is traceable from prior event to replacement
invalid or withdrawn event remains auditable
consumers know whether to show historical, advisory, blocking, or replacement posture

artifacts.usage: Explicit deferred surfaces for event correction and supersession semantics.

rows: append-only semantics require preserving original events and adding correction records
not allowed for append-only stable event surfaces
storage, ordering, retention, and delivery behavior depends on implementation infrastructure
rebuilding read models requires runtime execution and migration policy beyond static metadata
whether a correction reason is sufficient remains owned by domain authority and human review

artifacts.usage: Field map for the implementation topology pilot.

rows: id, locator, role, branch, status, notes
repo_id, paths, status, pr_refs, authority_surface, evidence[], acceptance_criteria[]
landed in U-009; evidence landed in U-027; acceptance criteria landed in U-029
total, satisfied, planned, partial, waived, blocked, manual_review
linked_modules, unlinked_modules, checked_paths, missing_paths, evidence_total, checked_evidence_locators, missing_evidence_locators, unchecked_reason
landed in U-010; evidence counters landed in U-027

artifacts.usage: Minimum fields for artifact-level decision provenance boundaries.

rows: agent-consumable, human-review, or internal decision surface
source-evidence, derived-summary, or mixed decision basis
sections or artifacts that authorize the decision
sections or artifacts that prove or audit the decision
section or artifact that owns the derived summary
current, stale, unresolved, or withheld evidence posture
stable, review-required, blocked, or experimental consumption rule
review anchors that justify consuming a derived decision

artifacts.usage: Classify stable-surface changes before version compatibility review.

rows: deprecate first or provide migration guidance
treat as new contract or versioned migration

artifacts.usage: Minimum field guide for adapter-facing capability claims.

rows: stable identifier for the adapter or provider surface
named capability such as route, validate, export, or query
local-only, hosted, or hybrid execution posture
tells consumers whether the claim is remote, local, or mixed
keeps adapter claims tied to an existing contract model
strict, versioned, or experimental compatibility posture
tells consumers how capability changes should be interpreted
tells consumers how stale the capability claim may become
sensitive-data handling posture for capability output
prevents stable adapter claims from bypassing redaction governance
authority or evidence surface that justifies the capability claim
keeps adapter claims reviewable instead of marketing-shaped

artifacts.usage: Metadata-only boundary for capability negotiation re-triage.

rows: capability_id, contract_profile, schema_version_policy, transport_scope, freshness_posture, redaction_posture, limits, evidence_anchor
capability_id, accepted_contract_profile, required_schema_version_policy, transport_scope, freshness_requirement, redaction_floor, blocking
capability_id match, compatible profile, compatible schema policy, compatible transport, freshness meets requirement, redaction meets floor

artifacts.usage: Explicit deferred surfaces for capability negotiation.

rows: requires a protocol surface and trust boundary beyond static metadata
requires credential, scope, and transport security semantics
future security and adapter protocol task
requires executing provider behavior and defining retry or timeout posture
requires ranking, fallback, and conflict policy across multiple providers

artifacts.usage: Deterministic validation posture for stable-surface contract metadata.

rules.action: L2 error: redaction summary must be mirrored

rules.condition: module.meta.redaction.has_sensitive_payloads == true AND missing sensitive_classes or exposure_policy
rules.action: L2 error: sensitive modules must declare classes and exposure policy
L2 error: contract profile summary must be mirrored
L2 error: stable read-model contracts must declare freshness and watermark metadata

rules.condition: stable write-capable command surface claims auditability but lacks command, receipt, event_or_projection, or explicit not-applicable linkage
rules.action: future L3 warning: write-capable command surfaces should expose triad linkage before stable consumption

rules.condition: stable append-only event surface claims auditability but lacks correction, supersession, retraction, or projection guidance semantics
rules.action: future L3 warning: append-only event surfaces should expose correction and supersession posture before stable consumption
L2 error: schema versioning summary must be mirrored
L2 error: implementation linkage summary must be mirrored
L2 error: implementation repository ids must be unique
L2 error: implementation repo linkage must resolve to manifest.project_topology.implementation_repos[]

rules.condition: module.meta.implementation.status == current AND module.meta.implementation.evidence[] is empty
rules.action: L2 error: current implementation linkage must declare evidence anchors

rules.condition: validate --reality can resolve implementation repo root AND path-like evidence locator is missing
rules.action: L2 error: path-like implementation evidence locators must exist

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance exists AND any source_refs target is unresolved
rules.action: L2 error: decision provenance source refs must resolve to sections or artifacts

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance exists AND any evidence_refs target is unresolved
rules.action: L2 error: decision provenance evidence refs must resolve to sections or artifacts

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance.summary_ref exists AND summary_ref target is unresolved
rules.action: L2 error: decision provenance summary_ref must resolve to a section or artifact

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance.basis is derived-summary or mixed AND summary_ref is missing
rules.action: L2 error: derived decision provenance must identify the summary owner

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance.consumer_surface == agent-consumable AND consumption_gate == stable AND evidence_status IN stale, unresolved, withheld
rules.action: L2 error: stable agent-consumable decisions require current evidence posture

changelog.delta: Initial stable-surface contract module for redaction, minimal profiles, topology pilot design, and version compatibility.
Landed root topology schema semantics and added the minimum adapter-facing capability contract boundary.
Landed module implementation linkage and topology-aware reality summary semantics for the design-to-reality pilot.
Clarified that implementation repository identifiers are unique topology identities.
Added declarative implementation evidence anchors, manifest evidence summaries, and reality locator checks for path-like evidence.
Added implementation acceptance criteria as contract-to-evidence linkage with manifest acceptance summaries.
Added artifact-level decision provenance boundaries for agent-consumable decisions and derived summaries.
Added read-model freshness and watermark metadata requirements for stable read-model contracts.
Re-triaged capability negotiation into metadata-only provider, consumer, compatibility matching, and evidence-link boundaries.
Added write-capable command receipt event triad boundaries for command audit linkage.
Added append-only event correction and supersession boundaries for stable event surfaces.

read_model.absence_semantics: Missing sections mean the profile is not defined in this module.
read_model.failure_semantics: Validation fails when required profile metadata is absent.
read_model.partial_semantics: Partial snapshots must not be consumed as stable contract authority.

schema_versioning.compatibility_window: minor-version line until deprecation guidance says otherwise
